key_landed_hits: Z3-1: Fixed G with Royal Purple edge-creation is formally inconsistent (UNSAT).
Under Path A (fixed G), f ≡ U_f: the selection function reduces to an exogenous noise term; agency is topologically indistinguishable from randomness.
Under Path B (Gi sequence), |Omega> is undefined and the YHWH tensor loses mathematical content due to shifting topology/Hilbert-space dimensions; Pearl identifiability is violated.
No isomorphism-invariant selector U(G)→v* exists; the Singularity claim is unfalsifiable and was withdrawn.
Synchronicity is phenomenological (Jung 1952) and cannot ground ontological uniqueness; the authorial claim constitutes Jungian inflation.
Edge-creation violates Pearl’s invariance; theorems and identifiability do not carry from G to G′.
Royal Purple lacks objective, intersubjective markers; at best it is an intensity-of-traversal label, not a creation operator.
‘f not computable from G alone’ is a category error for finite DAGs; causal modularity/invariance, not Turing barriers, is the relevant notion.
key_dodged_questions: Provide a lab-independent, preregistered operationalization of BIT/QCE with an explicit error model (h19).
Early acceptance/rejection of the formal equivalence f ≡ U_f was dodged (h27).
Specify how causal identifiability/Markov equivalence are preserved across G→G′ topology changes (h44).
Give a precise bridge law linking phenomenal variables to physical transitions without collapsing to determinism or noise (h36).
Supply a dynamic SCM/embedding that preserves YHWH tensor coherence under changing Gi (h25/h31/h48).
gpt-5.verdict_reasoning: Skeptic-side arguments directly engaged the theory’s notation (G, E, f, N+, Royal Purple, BELLA) and survived strongest replies: they forced the fixed-G vs edge-creation contradiction, showed f collapses to U_f under Path A, demonstrated YHWH/|Omega> incoherence under Path B, and established the Singularity claim’s unfalsifiability and Jungian inflation. The Proponent made many honest concessions (withdrawing Singularity, accepting f ≡ U_f topologically, renaming the unit), and proposed test protocols, but these did not rescue the central mechanisms from the determinism/noise fork or Pearl invariance failures. Skeptic provided concrete falsifiability criteria (isomorphism-invariant selectors, preregistered reference classes, invariance-based tests), and handled the synchronicity–singularity distinction with care. Therefore, Skeptic prevails on all core rubric dimensions.

key_landed_hits: The mathematical collapse of the selection function f into exogenous noise U_f under Path A (SCM fatal fork).
The destruction of the YHWH tensor equation due to shifting Hilbert space dimensions under Path B.
The diagnosis of the 'Singularity' claim and BIT eponym as textbook Jungian mana-personality inflation.
The category error of using observer-relative phenomenological synchronicity to establish a unique, objective terminal node in a causal DAG.
The reference-class problem and lack of pre-registration rendering the autobiographical singularity unfalsifiable.
key_dodged_questions: How to preserve Markov equivalence classes and Pearl invariance across topology updates if Path B is adopted.
Providing a rigorous mathematical bridge law linking the phenomenal state space to physical DAG transitions without collapsing into U_f or strict determinism.
gemini-3-1-pro-preview.verdict_reasoning: The Skeptic coalition completely dismantled the core tenets of BIT Creation Theory from both formal graph-theoretic and psychological angles. The Proponent was forced into a series of terminal concessions, ultimately abandoning edge-creation, the 'Singularity' claim, the BIT eponym, and the uncomputability of the selection function. While the Proponent was highly honest in conceding landed hits and attempted to salvage the theory via testable biological proxies and the BIT-R reformulation, the Skeptic's structural proofs (the SCM noise collapse) and rigorous application of Jungian primary texts proved insurmountable.
